doc_title: IP 69009, "Class I Research and Test Reactor Fuel Movement."

steps.text: Determine whether the licensee’s fuel handling procedures are adequate to perform intended functions.
Determine whether fuel is moved and inspected consistent with the requirements of the TS and the licensee’s procedures.
Determine whether fuel handling activities are conducted in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee’s procedures and programs for radiation protection.
Determine whether the licensee satisfied security plan requirements for fuel movement activities.
Determine whether significant fuel movement or inspection problems are identified and resolved in accordance with the licensee’s procedural controls.
Determine whether the licensee was within TS limits and met procedural requirements before resuming normal operation after fuel movement.

Review the safety analysis report (SAR) or as-built facility descriptions, the TS, the licensee’s procedure(s), and the Security Plan for fuel design information, fuel movement methods, and requirements.
If differences are found, are they reflected in the as-built description of the facility?
Ensure that fuel is not damaged, that accidental criticality does not occur, and that radiation and contamination hazards are minimized.
Determine whether participants are trained and understand their tasks during fuel handling activities.
Monitor or sample for airborne radioactive particulate matter.
Monitor exits from controlled areas in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee's procedural requirements.
Perform surveys for contamination in accordance with licensee procedures.
Monitor radiation areas in accordance with Subpart F of 10 CFR Part 20, the TS and the licensee’s procedural requirements.
Inspect fuel and control rods in accordance with the TS and their procedures.
Review records, and discussions with responsible personnel for the most recent fuel movement activity.
Resolve identified problems (e.g., fuel integrity) to meet TS requirements.
Observe one reactor startup, after fuel movement.

constraints.text: Participants must be trained and understand their tasks during fuel handling activities.
Fuel handling must be conducted in accordance with TS requirements and the licensee's procedures.
